META_DESCRIPTION: KoalaPull is a native desktop app for yt-dlp. Download videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata on macOS, Windows, and Linux without terminal friction.
OG_DESCRIPTION: Paste a link, pick a format, and let KoalaPull handle yt-dlp with a clean native desktop flow.
TWITTER_DESCRIPTION: A native desktop app for yt-dlp. Pull videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata without terminal friction.
HERO_SUBTITLE: Paste a link, choose a format, and let KoalaPull handle videos, audio, playlists, subtitles, and metadata with a native app instead of terminal commands.
HERO_NOTE: KoalaPull keeps the power of yt-dlp close: pull what you want with KoalaPull.
HERO_STAT_1_TEXT: KoalaPull downloads yt-dlp and ffmpeg for you. No manual setup dance.
HERO_STAT_2_TEXT: Preview metadata, choose the right output, and keep control over where files go.
HERO_STAT_3_TEXT: Run one link or a full playlist with live status, clean presets, and visible history.
FEATURES_SUBTITLE: A desktop flow that makes yt-dlp feel obvious instead of hidden behind flags and copy-pasted commands.
FEATURE_1_DESC: KoalaPull downloads yt-dlp and ffmpeg into your app data folder on first launch, so setup stays short and local.
FEATURE_2_DESC: See title, uploader, duration, formats, and estimated size before you commit a job.
FEATURE_3_DESC: Start with best quality, compatible output, or audio-only, then switch to custom when you need more control.
FEATURE_4_DESC: Load up several jobs, keep them moving, and watch progress without losing the bigger picture.
FEATURE_5_DESC: Pull single clips or bigger batches, then track what finished, when it ran, and how fast it moved.
FEATURE_6_DESC: No account, no telemetry, no CDN dependency for the site, and no need to live in a terminal window.
MOCKUP_SUBTITLE: See the desktop flow before you download.
MOCKUP_TEXT: Click through the screens, queue a fake job, switch presets, and get a quick feel for how KoalaPull looks and behaves.
MOCK_DOWNLOADS_FETCH_HELP: Fake scan. Real button. Click it to simulate metadata lookup before the queue starts.
MOCK_TOAST_DONE: Demo finished. Open folder would fire now.
MOCK_TOAST_RESET: Demo reset. Queue back to idle.
MOCK_SETTINGS_THEME_DESC: Switch the feel of the app without hiding the important bits.
MOCK_SETTINGS_LANGUAGE_DESC: Move between supported locales without leaving the app shell.
MOCK_SETTINGS_OUTPUT_DESC: Keep finished files where you actually expect them.
MOCK_SETTINGS_CONCURRENCY_DESC: Set how aggressive the queue should feel.
MOCK_SETTINGS_AUTOPASTE_DESC: Optional clipboard pickup for supported links.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_1: Paste a video or playlist URL into Downloads.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_2: Analyze the link and check the preview.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_3: Choose a preset or custom output.
MOCK_HELP_STEP_4: Queue the job and watch the progress state shift.
MOCK_HELP_SITES_TEXT: YouTube, Vimeo, Twitch, TikTok, SoundCloud, Bandcamp, and hundreds more through yt-dlp.
HOWTO_DESC: Simple path. Copy link. Pick output. Pull media with KoalaPull.
HOWTO_STEP_1_TEXT: Drop in one video link or a playlist URL from your browser.
HOWTO_STEP_2_TEXT: Inspect title, uploader, duration, and available formats before you start.
HOWTO_STEP_3_TEXT: Use the fast preset or switch to audio-only and custom output when needed.
HOWTO_STEP_4_TEXT: Run one job or several and watch progress without juggling terminal windows.
HOWTO_STEP_5_TEXT: When the job finishes, jump straight to the output folder and move on.
FAQ_SUBTITLE: Short answers to the first questions people usually have.
FAQ_Q1: Is KoalaPull free and open source?
FAQ_A1: Yes. KoalaPull is open source under the MIT License and free to use.
FAQ_Q2: Do I need yt-dlp or ffmpeg installed first?
FAQ_A2: No. KoalaPull downloads the required tools into your app data folder on first launch.
FAQ_A3: KoalaPull uses yt-dlp, so it can work with hundreds of supported sites, including YouTube, Vimeo, Twitch, SoundCloud, and more.
FAQ_Q4: Can it handle playlists and subtitles?
FAQ_A4: Yes. Playlist downloads, subtitle options, and metadata previews are built into the app.
FAQ_A5: Local-first and privacy-friendly. No telemetry, no account, and no extra hosted script dependencies on the site.
FAQ_Q6: Why use KoalaPull instead of the terminal?
FAQ_A6: It keeps the power of yt-dlp, but adds buttons, previews, history, presets, and queue control in a native desktop flow.
CTA_TITLE: Ready to pull your next download?
CTA_TEXT: Grab the latest release or inspect the code on GitHub. KoalaPull is for people who want yt-dlp power without terminal friction.
FOOTER_MIT: Open source under the MIT License.
IMPRINT_META_DESCRIPTION: Legal notice and responsibility information for KoalaPull.
IMPRINT_PROVIDER_BODY: KoalaPull is maintained by Timo (KoalaDev) as a private, open-source project.
IMPRINT_DDG_BODY: This site is a private, non-commercial open-source project. If mandatory provider identification under Section 5 DDG applies to a concrete use of this site, the required details must be provided separately by the responsible operator.
IMPRINT_APP_USE_BODY: KoalaPull is a graphical interface for yt-dlp. Users are solely responsible for the links they process, the files they download, and their later use of those files. Users must check whether downloading or storing specific media is legal in their country and whether it is allowed by copyright law, platform terms, licences, and other applicable rules.
IMPRINT_CONTENT_BODY: We take care to keep our own content accurate, but we do not give legal, copyright, or platform-specific advice. KoalaPull is provided without warranty under the MIT License.
IMPRINT_LINKS_BODY: This website links to external services such as GitHub and project dependencies. We do not control third-party content and are not responsible for it. If we become aware of unlawful content behind an external link, we will remove that link.
IMPRINT_COPYRIGHT_BODY: Website text, design, and project materials created by the project are protected by copyright unless a licence says otherwise. KoalaPull source code is available under the MIT License.
PRIVACY_META_DESCRIPTION: Privacy information for the KoalaPull website and app.
PRIVACY_CONTROLLER_BODY: The controller for this website is the KoalaPull project maintainer, Timo (KoalaDev).
PRIVACY_WEBSITE_BODY: When you visit this static website, your browser necessarily sends technical request data such as your IP address, requested file, date and time, user agent, and referrer if supplied by your browser. We use this data only for delivering the website and maintaining security. We do not keep website logs.
PRIVACY_APP_BODY: KoalaPull does not send telemetry, analytics, download URLs, media metadata, history, settings, or downloaded files back to us. The app works locally on your device. If KoalaPull downloads yt-dlp, ffmpeg, or update information from third-party sources, those requests go to the respective third-party service, not to us.
PRIVACY_NO_TRACKING_BODY: We do not use analytics tools, advertising networks, tracking pixels, tracking cookies, external CDNs, externally hosted fonts, or third-party scripts on this website.
PRIVACY_LEGAL_BASIS_BODY: The legal basis for the short technical processing of request data is our legitimate interest under Article 6(1)(f) GDPR: delivering a secure and functional website. No consent banner is needed because we do not set non-essential cookies or use tracking.
PRIVACY_RECIPIENTS_BODY: We do not sell visitor data, share it for advertising, or transfer it to analytics providers. Technical request data is processed only for the time needed to deliver the website. We do not keep website logs.
PRIVACY_RIGHTS_BODY: Under the GDPR, you may have rights to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, objection, data portability, and complaint to a supervisory authority. Because we do not keep visitor profiles or logs, we usually cannot identify a visitor after a request has ended.
